message:
cx/scope: catch panics from the child-scope future constructor

Wrap the `f(child_scope, &mut *state)` call inside
`Scope::scope_with` in `std::panic::catch_unwind` using
`AssertUnwindSafe`, so a panic raised while *constructing* the
child future — rather than while polling it — does not leave the
scope state partially initialized.

The existing `CatchUnwind` wrapper only covers the *polling* path
of the returned future. If the construction closure itself panics
(for example, an `unwrap()` on a pre-computed input or a tracing
macro that panics inside its init), the scope previously unwound
through the `child_region` bookkeeping without ever marking that
region as cancelled or closed, which could strand a region in the
active set of `state` and interfere with later region id
allocation and liveness checks.

The new catch_unwind path, on panic:

1. Builds a `CancelReason::fail_fast().with_region(child_region)`
   so downstream observers can attribute the cancellation to a
   scope constructor panic.
2. Calls `state.cancel_request(child_region, &reason, None)` to
   mark the region as cancel-requested (return value ignored — the
   region may already be cancelled via parent propagation).
3. Calls `region.begin_close(None)` via `state.region(child_region)`
   to move the region into the closing state so the scheduler
   will drain it.
4. Calls `state.advance_region_state(child_region)` to push the
   region through its close-state transitions synchronously
   before we resume the panic.
5. Resumes the panic via `std::panic::resume_unwind(payload)` so
   the caller's panic semantics are preserved exactly — we are
   not swallowing the error, only fencing the region bookkeeping.

This closes the window where a panic inside the constructor could
leak a half-initialized region into the parent scope's state map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
